text: When you write code, you will often encounter bugs, or errors, that stop your program from working correctly. Identifying bugs is the first step in fixing them. A good strategy is to take a systematic approach: instead of guessing, try to narrow down where the problem occurs. You can use debugging tools, like breakpoints in your code editor, to pause the program and inspect variables. Another helpful method is logging—adding print statements to see what your code is doing at each stage. By combining these techniques, you can find and fix errors more efficiently, making your debugging process much less frustrating.
